headlines.title: Kentucky governor urges Mitch McConnell to be ‘transparent’ and give an update on his health
headlines.description: Kentucky Gov. Andy Beshear is asking Mitch McConnell for an update on his health and whether he believes he can continue to serve in the role.

headlines.title: Democratic fighting escalates in Maine as Graham Platner stalls on making a decision
headlines.description: More than 48 hours after Graham Platner signaled he may drop out of the Maine Senate race, he continues to hold off on announcing whether he’ll end his campaign.

headlines.title: Democrat running to replace Platner says latest allegations crossed his ‘bright red line’
headlines.description: Fmr. State Sen. Troy Jackson (D-Maine) joins Meet the Press NOW to discuss the new sexual assault allegations against Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner and why he's entering the race to replace the candidate he once supported on the campaign trail.

headlines.title: Iran hit by round of U.S. strikes after Trump gives ‘mixed messaging’ on scope of military conflict
headlines.description: The U.S. military launches new strikes against Iran after President Trump suggested the ceasefire is “over." NBC News' Monica Alba, Brian Cheung, Courtney Kube and “Cosmopolitics” podcast host Elise Labott join Meet the Press NOW to discuss the state of the war.

headlines.title: Trump faces questions on conflict with Iran during NATO summit press conference
headlines.description: President Trump held a news conference capping off the latest NATO summit in Turkey and faced questions from reporters about the ongoing conflict with Iran. NBC News' Garrett Haake and Courtney Kube report on the latest developments in negotiations and strikes in the Middle East.

headlines.title: State Assemblyman Alex Bores speaks on AI and politics
headlines.description: State Assemblyman Alex Bores, a candidate for New York's 12th Congressional District, speaks with NBC News’ Gadi Schwartz about why some top artificial intelligence companies are opposing his candidacy and whether AI should play a role in politics.

headlines.title: Judge orders release of the $5.8 million payment that Trump owed E. Jean Carroll
headlines.description: A federal judge ruled Wednesday that E. Jean Carroll can receive the $5.8 million awarded to her after a 2023 jury found that President Donald Trump sexually abused her in 1996 and later defamed her when he denied her allegations.

headlines.title: Senate sets new date for Jay Clayton’s confirmation hearing after Trump derailed the previous one
headlines.description: Clayton’s June 17 hearing for director of national intelligence was postponed at the last minute after President Donald Trump intervened.

headlines.title: Tracking the DOJ's effort to get U.S. voter registration data
headlines.description: The Justice Department is seeking voter registration list data from all 50 states and the District of Columbia over the past year.

headlines.title: DOJ warns of criminal charges for state election officials if noncitizens vote
headlines.description: President Donald Trump and his administration have falsely claimed that noncitizen voting is a widespread problem plaguing U.S. elections.

headlines.title: Graham Platner considering ‘best path forward’ in Maine Senate race after denying sexual assault claim
headlines.description: Jenny Racicot, who once dated Platner, said he showed up at her home in 2021 and forced himself on her. Platner said the allegation is “categorically false.”

headlines.title: Trump says he asked FIFA to review U.S. soccer star Folarin Balogun’s suspension
headlines.description: The striker’s red card prompted fury from American soccer fans and concerns about the team’s prospects without its star player.
